C2 Attitude: He can work independently at times however he often gets distracted if kids are nearby. He struggles with feeling overwhelmed a lot with most work.
C2 Health: He is high functioning Autistic with a PDA profile and has ADHD. We have worked with him a lot and he often masks if he is struggling. However, if he is struggling too much he may get frustrated with teachers or other kids. Normally, he will just wait until he gets in the car and freaks out. C2 Hopes: It is a great enrichment to what we teach at home and gives him opportunities to make friends.

Parent 1 Address: 4405 N Murray Dr, Otis Orchards, Washington, 99027
C1 Attitude: Kieran is a focused and dedicated student. He usually understands concepts quickly and can tend to be bored when not challenged. When things do challenge him he finds a lot of satisfaction in accomplishing them. It is important to him to complete his work on time. He works well independently and in a group, often in a group he tends to fall into a leadership position.
C1 Hopes: We hope that at River Tech Kieran will grow both academically and socially, providing a well-rounded environment to help him flourish. We hope he will make good friends that will be a positive aspect of his life. We also hope he will be challenged academically and enjoy pursuing his interests.
C1 Notes:
Kieran is a talented artist. He loves to draw both on paper and digitally. He also enjoys pyrography (wood burning), painting figurines and creative writing. Kieran plays the piano and has started to teach himself guitar.

Kieran is the oldest of our six children. Two of our younger boys have congenital health conditions. While God has helped our family navigate through these challenges, they have inevitably impacted our life emotionally, spiritually and socially. We are hopeful that if Kieran is accepted he will benefit from his experience at River Tech and that it will provide the opportunity to make friends and use the gifts that God has given him to find purpose, joy and grow as a young adult.

Parent 1 Address: 1530 N Moonstone St, Post Falls, Idaho, 83854
993 W. Cordgrass Ave, Post Falls, Idaho, 83854
C1 Attitude: Emersyn enjoys learning. She is very good at  getting work done. She does like and need very clear directions and what is expected. She also likes and needs to be able to ask questions.
C1 Hopes: Emersyn as well as our family are looking for more and new opportunity to learn and grow from others. Gain new friends and knowledge from peers and teachers.
C1 Notes: Emersyn loves to try new things and be helpful. She danced for years mostly in classical ballet until she hurt her knee. She still loves to dance and sing all the time. She likes to ask questions and get as many details as she can. We are very much looking for a like minded community to be welcomed into and become part of. We just relocating here from Star,ID (Boise area) a month ago.  After trying some different classes and options down there we had some tough experiences at, we are nervous trying somewhere new but optimistic .
C2 Attitude: Annistyn loves to learn new information. She can work well on her own for awhile but needs clear directions. She can get bored and likes to be busy with her hands most of the time. Talking about what she is learning or doing with others helps her a lot. She likes small short goals to get done.
C2 Hopes: New experiences, new friends, work on confidence in learning.
C2 Notes: Annistyn is very artistic and very active. She loves to learn new things and be helpful. Art is her favorite thing.  She will paint anything and she also loves to draw and is very good at it. She did dance for awhile as well. Currently she is struggling reading and writing. Things she is not confident  in but we are slowly working on it. We recently moved here from Star,ID (Boise area). We had some tough experiences with classes down there and all my kids are very nervous to even look at a new one but also hope to find something fun. We truthfully are looking for a new community where we feel welcome, safe and happy. We love homeschooling but also would like our children to try new things we cannot always do at home. We are looking at one day right now for Annistyn and her twin brother Jameson to see how they do. If they are happy we would be interested in more days in the future.
C3 Attitude: Jameson is a smart guy. He loves to learn but is very much hands on. He can get work done well he understands what he's doing and has clear directions.
C3 Health: He has severe asthma and environmental allergy's. It is always something we need to be watching for.  He is very sensitive to smoke as well of any kind whether on people or in the air. He loves to be active but has limits. Some days he's fine while others are much harder. He needs to be able to stop and get his inhaler whenever he  feels necessary. Also, I would like the teachers to be made aware to be mindful of this just so he can be guided to grab his inhaler if he seems or looks off. Sadly, we have had times where people do not understand the severity of his asthma and refuse to let him take a break and use it. So we want to be clear and are very cautious. Thank you for your understanding.
C3 Hopes: New friends, positive experiences with teachers and growth with confidence.
C3 Notes: Jameson is a calm boy who likes to have fun and be silly at the same time. He likes all the little details in things and finding out how things work. He loves to make and create things such a as building. He is a sensitive little guy who wears his heart on his sleeve. He always wants to make people happy. Currently he is struggling with writing but we are working with him to improve as possible. We just moved here from Star,ID (Boise area). We tried school/classes last year down south and it was a very tough experiences for Jameson. Unfortunately, they had the kids climb trees to fight their fears. However, they refused to help them. Jameson got stuck 10 feet up and they let him fall. He was hurt of course but they would still do nothing to help him and jet let him lay on the ground and only tell him to calm down for 20 minutes until we got there. Obviously, this was a very difficult issue and it had a strong effect on him, our family and our trust with people and teachers around our kids. Thankfully he's okay. We know there are great teachers but have yet to find them in a place that works well for our family. Of course, the safety and well being of all three of our kiddos is top priority. We know Jameson will enjoy the productions on the Monday schedule. However, he does not like to be the center of attention. He would very much like to be involved in things such a as scenes or background things like he saw in one of the videos. My hope is he will warm up to more as time goes on without being pushed and his confidence grows. If all goes well, we would be interested in adding more days in the future. Thank you for your time.
